title: Heilind Asia Pacific Highlights Automotive Manufacturing and Supply Chain Solutions in Bangkok with TE Connectivity, Molex and Heyco
author: Not Specified
description: Heilind Asia Pacific will exhibit at Automotive Manufacturing 2026 in Bangkok (June 17-20) showcasing interconnect, fastening, and cable management solutions for automotive manufacturing. The company will be joined by partner suppliers TE Connectivity, Molex, and Heyco to demonstrate technologies addressing industry trends toward electrification, automation, and connectivity.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as a partner supplier participating in the exhibition. No specific performance metrics or business developments disclosed regarding TE Connectivity itself.

title: TE Connectivity declares quarterly dividend
author: Prnewswire
description: TE Connectivity plc (NYSE:TEL) announced a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.78 per ordinary share, payable on September 11, 2026, to shareholders of record as of August 21, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The declaration of a regular quarterly dividend demonstrates the company's financial health and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Consistent dividend payments are typically viewed positively by investors as they indicate stable cash flows and management confidence in the company's future performance.

title: 3 Great Value AI Stocks (Hint: 1 is a Household Name; the Other 2 You've Never Heard Of)
author: Lee Samaha
description: The article identifies three AI stocks offering good value: Alphabet (Google), which is investing heavily in AI infrastructure with potential for strong future free cash flow; Brady Corporation, benefiting from rapid data center-related wire identification product growth and the upcoming Honeywell PSS acquisition; and Belden, positioned to benefit from both data center buildout through 2030 and subsequent inference spending growth, with the RUCKUS Networks acquisition expanding its enterprise networking exposure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong AI infrastructure investment positioning with projected steady 30% FCF margins and $1 trillion+ revenue by 2030s, potentially reaching $6.7 trillion valuation. Capital spending expected to moderate as data center buildout completes.

Strong AI infrastructure investment positioning with projected steady 30% FCF margins and $1 trillion+ revenue by 2030s, potentially reaching $6.7 trillion valuation. Capital spending expected to moderate as data center buildout completes.

Data center wire identification products growing 19% and 13% across regions, significantly outpacing overall company growth. Honeywell PSS acquisition is transformative, combining mobile scanning expertise with labeling capabilities. Trading at attractive 14.4x 2027 earnings.

Well-positioned for both data center buildout through 2030 and inference spending growth thereafter. RUCKUS Networks acquisition increases enterprise networking exposure. Trading at significant discount to peers like TE Connectivity and Amphenol despite double-digit data center exposure growth.
Mentioned as a peer comparison point; trades at premium valuation relative to Belden despite similar data center exposure, suggesting less attractive value proposition.

title: Tractor Supply, Lululemon, and Northrop Grumman Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Losers Last Week (April 20-April 24): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Large-cap stocks experienced significant selling pressure during the week of April 20-24, 2026, driven by earnings disappointments, cautious guidance, and analyst downgrades. Ten major companies saw sharp declines, with Charter Communications leading losses at 24.78%, followed by Medpace at 20.8%, and Tractor Supply at 18.54%. Other notable decliners included Lululemon, Northrop Grumman, Lockheed Martin, and TE Connectivity, all falling between 13-14%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Largest weekly decline at 24.78% due to worse-than-expected Q1 EPS results

title: SATA HDD Connector Market Trends and Opportunities Worldwide, 2026-2032
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global SATA HDD connector market is projected to grow from USD 1.03 billion in 2026 to USD 1.47 billion by 2032, with a CAGR of 5.93%. Growth is driven by sustained adoption of standardized storage solutions across enterprise, surveillance, industrial, and consumer platforms, despite emerging new protocols. Key trends include increased focus on reliability, compliance, automation-ready designs, and dual-source supply strategies to mitigate regional disruptions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key company in the growing SATA HDD connector market with strong demand across enterprise, surveillance, and industrial sectors. Market growth of 5.93% CAGR through 2032 presents expansion opportunities.
Profiled as a major player in the SATA HDD connector market. Increasing demand for reliable connectors in mission-critical systems and automation-ready designs supports positive outlook.

Included as a key company in the SATA HDD connector ecosystem. Sustained adoption of SATA interfaces in enterprise and consumer storage platforms supports continued relevance and market opportunities.
